settings.text: 30% of the labour cost, max 50,000 kr/person/year
We handle the application to the Swedish Tax Agency
The deduction is visible directly on your invoice

settings.question: How much ROT deduction do I get on a heat pump?
settings.answer: You get 30% of the labour cost, max 50,000 kr per person and year. Two owners can share the deduction up to 100,000 kr.

settings.question: Do I get ROT deduction on the materials?
settings.answer: No, ROT only applies to the labour cost. The materials (the heat pump, pipes, drilling) are not included in the deduction.

settings.question: Do I need to apply myself?
settings.answer: No, we deduct ROT directly on the invoice and handle the application to the Swedish Tax Agency on your behalf.

settings.question: What are the conditions?
settings.answer: You must own the property and be an unlimited tax resident in Sweden. Housing associations cannot use ROT, but members can in their own apartment for certain types of work.

settings.heading: ROT deduction on heat pump installation
settings.description: You get 30% off the labour cost when you install a heat pump – up to 50,000 kr per person and year. We deduct it directly on the invoice.
settings.how_description: The ROT deduction is a tax reduction for repair, conversion and extension work. Installation of a heat pump in your own home is covered by the rules.
settings.cta_text: Get a quote with ROT included
settings.faq_heading: Frequently asked questions about ROT deduction
